prose:
**Property** — Condensed Fukui reactivity descriptors (f+, f−, dual descriptor) for acrolein (CH2=CH–CHO, 8 atoms), from finite-difference of Mulliken atomic charges (PySCF Mulliken scheme) across the neutral, cation (N−1), and anion (N+1) states.

**Method / level of theory** — DFT, tier `standard` = **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (PySCF, `dft` backend). Exact four-center two-electron integrals (density fitting OFF by default). Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral parent, closed-shell RKS). The N±1 states were computed at cation charge +1 / multiplicity 2 and anion charge −1 / multiplicity 2 (UKS). **Gas phase** (no implicit solvent; `--solvent none`). I explicitly passed `--accept-defaults` to consent to the gas-phase default; the functional, basis, and tier were all specified as you requested.

**Geometry provenance** — User-supplied geometry `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz`, used as-is (single-point Fukui evaluation; no geometry optimization performed at this level).

**Software** — PySCF via the chemkit `fukui` engine.

**Results (dimensionless Fukui indices, per atom; atom order O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H):**
- Most **electrophilic** site (largest f+): atom 4 (1-based), a **carbon**, with **f+ = 0.64232** — this is the headline value reported.
- Most **nucleophilic** site (largest f−): atom 1 (1-based), the **oxygen**, with f− = 0.39389.
- f+ per atom: [0.40136, 0.022255, 0.43405, 0.64232, −0.15726, −0.20720, −0.062117, −0.073408]
- f− per atom: [0.39389, 0.0059884, 0.091403, 0.087370, 0.085961, 0.18568, 0.084486, 0.065226]
- dual descriptor (f+ − f−): [0.0074780, 0.016267, 0.34264, 0.55495, −0.24322, −0.39288, −0.14660, −0.13863] — the large positive dual descriptor at C4 (0.55495) marks it as the preferred site for nucleophilic attack, consistent with the β-carbon of the enone system.

**Convergence / integrity** — Engine integrity status: **ok**, trustworthy: **true**. All integrity checks passed: Fukui charge conservation sum(f+) = 1.0000 and sum(f−) = 1.0000 (both ~1.0 as expected, indicating the N±1 states converged), and schema header present.

**Warnings** — No warnings were reported (the result JSON contained no `warnings` array; all integrity checks passed with status ok).

**Reproducibility** — CLI invocation: `cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults --stdout json <acrolein.xyz> --out .../live.json`. Result JSON written to `acrolein_fukui_dft.json`; bar-chart PNG at `.../20260704-044647_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.

Note on precision: Fukui indices are condensed-charge finite-difference descriptors and are Mulliken-population-dependent (basis-set sensitive); they are best used as relative site-reactivity trends rather than absolute quantities. No experimental/literature comparison is offered.
